mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index signals contemporaneous manufacturing expansion that translates into higher near-term corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth months are negative on average over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0 percent indicates significant labor market slack that depresses consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal accelerating monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative average 20-day returns in walk-forward testing, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles identified by a declining federal funds rate over the prior three months, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects strengthening aggregate demand that is amplified by accommodative credit conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified easing cycles, positive GDPC1 growth does not coincide with positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the regime-stratified Sharpe ratio is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts exceeding 20 thousand units signal accelerating construction activity and spillover demand for durable goods and credit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST increases greater than 20K units are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decrease in the unemployment rate and increase in nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts aggregate consumption forecasts and cyclical profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS does not produce positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that elevates borrowing costs and reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment above 6 percent, month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal severe demand shortfalls and capacity underutilization that drive corporate earnings revisions lower, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 6 percent, negative INDPRO growth does not coincide with negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is above zero.
